Analogies.theory: Analogy tests measure your ability to identify the relationship between a pair of words and apply that same relationship to another pair. Look for cause-effect, part-whole, synonym, antonym, tool-use, or category relationships.
formulas: Identify the relationship in the given pair
Apply the same relationship to find the missing word
Types: Synonyms, Antonyms, Part:Whole, Tool:User, Cause:Effect

questions.q: Book : Library :: Painting : ?
Doctor : Hospital :: Teacher : ?
Fish : Water :: Bird : ?
Pen : Write :: Knife : ?
Sun : Day :: Moon : ?

Series Completion.theory: Series completion involves identifying the pattern in a sequence of numbers, letters, or figures and finding the next term. Look for arithmetic, geometric, alternating, or Fibonacci-type patterns.
formulas: Arithmetic series: add/subtract a constant
Geometric series: multiply/divide by a constant
Square/Cube series: terms are perfect squares or cubes
Mixed: combination of two operations alternately

questions.q: A, C, E, G, ?

Blood Relations.theory: Blood relation problems test your ability to decode family relationships from given statements. Draw a family tree to map relationships clearly.
formulas: Draw a family tree for complex problems
Father/Mother → Parent
Son/Daughter → Child
Brother/Sister → Sibling
Uncle/Aunt → Parent's Sibling

questions.q: A is B's father. B is C's son. What is A to C?
X's mother is Y's daughter. How is Y related to X?
P is Q's brother. Q is R's sister. How is P related to R?
If A is B's sister's husband, what is A to B?
M's father is N's son. M is N's?

Direction Sense.theory: Direction sense tests involve tracking movement in various directions and finding final position or distance from start. Always start by fixing the starting point and tracking each move step by step.
formulas: Directions: North, South, East, West
Right turn from North → East
Left turn from North → West
Pythagoras theorem for diagonal distance
Draw the path on paper to avoid errors

questions.q: A walks 5km North, then 5km East. Distance from start?
Facing North, turn right. Which direction now?
A goes 10km South, 5km West, 10km North. Where is A relative to start?
Facing West, turn right twice. Now facing?
A walks 3km East, 4km North. Distance from start?

Coding-Decoding.theory: Coding-Decoding tests check if you can identify the rule used to encode a word or number and apply it to decode or encode another. Look for letter shifts, reversals, number substitution, or symbol-based patterns.
formulas: Letter shift: each letter moved +N or -N positions in alphabet
Reverse coding: word or letters written backwards
Position-based: A=1, B=2, ... Z=26
Pattern-based: odd positions/even positions coded differently

questions.q: If CAT = 3120, DOG = ?
If APPLE is coded as BQQMF, how is MANGO coded?
If BOOK = 2151511, what is COD?
If FACE is coded as HCEG, how is BALL coded?
If SUN = 19-21-14, what is SKY?

Syllogism.theory: Syllogism involves drawing conclusions from given statements (premises) using logical reasoning. Use Venn diagrams to visualize relationships between sets.
formulas: All A are B + All B are C → All A are C
Some A are B + All B are C → Some A are C
No A is B + All C are A → No C is B
Use Venn diagrams to visualize overlaps
Check: Definite vs Possible conclusions

questions.q: All cats are animals. All animals have hearts. Conclusion?
Some books are pens. All pens are pencils. Conclusion?
No dog is cat. All cats are animals. Conclusion?
All roses are flowers. Some flowers are red. Conclusion?
All men are mortal. Socrates is a man. Conclusion?

Odd One Out.theory: Odd One Out tests require identifying which item does not belong to the group. Items can be classified by category, property, number pattern, or letter pattern.
formulas: Group by category (animals, fruits, vehicles, etc.)
Group by property (color, shape, size, usage)
Number groups: even/odd, prime/composite, squares
Letter groups: vowels/consonants, position patterns

questions.q: Apple, Mango, Carrot, Banana — Odd one out?
2, 3, 5, 6, 7 — Odd one out?
Tiger, Lion, Elephant, Cobra — Odd one out?
Chair, Table, Bed, Lamp — Odd one out?

Seating Arrangement.theory: Seating arrangement problems involve placing people in a line or circle based on given conditions. Read all conditions first, identify definite positions, then place others relative to them.
formulas: In a line: positions are fixed (1st, 2nd, etc.)
In a circle: relative positions matter, not absolute
Start with the most constrained person
Use elimination to place remaining people
For circular: one person's position is fixed as reference

questions.q: A, B, C sit in a row. A is left of B, C is right of B. Who is in the middle?
5 people sit in a circle. P is between Q and R. S is opposite P. Where is T?
In a row of 6, X is 2nd from left, Y is 4th from left. Who is between them?
A sits 3rd from left. B sits 2nd from right in 5 seats. How many between them?
In a row, M is 5th from left and 3rd from right. Total people?
